name: murphy_technical_analysis_knowledge_graph
source_basis: Derived as a condensed knowledge graph from the user's scanned Chinese edition of John J. Murphy's Technical Analysis of the Futures Markets, using OCR on the front matter and chapter opening material plus domain-structured decomposition. This file is a summary and workflow map, not a reproduction of the book.

core_assumptions.label: Market action discounts everything
aliases: 市场行为包容消化一切
core_assumptions.summary: Price, volume, and open interest are treated as the observable result of fundamental, psychological, political, and liquidity forces.

core_assumptions.label: Prices move in trends
core_assumptions.summary: The primary job of chart analysis is to detect and follow trends until evidence shows reversal or exhaustion.

core_assumptions.label: History tends to repeat
core_assumptions.summary: Recurring market psychology creates recurring structures, patterns, and indicator behaviors.

nodes.label: Technical analysis
nodes.summary: Study market action to assess probable future price direction and trading tactics.

nodes.label: Market action
nodes.summary: The primary observable dataset for chart analysis.

nodes.label: Price
nodes.summary: The main signal source; trend, structure, patterns, and indicators are derived from it.

nodes.label: Volume
nodes.summary: Participation evidence used to confirm breakouts, warn on weak moves, and build indicators such as OBV.

nodes.label: Open interest
nodes.summary: Futures participation evidence showing whether contracts are being opened or closed.

nodes.label: Dow Theory
nodes.summary: Classical foundation for trend classification, confirmation, and the idea that trends persist until reversal evidence appears.

nodes.label: Chart construction
nodes.summary: Choose bar/candle/line/point-and-figure chart, timeframe, scale, and data fields before interpreting signals.

nodes.label: Trend
nodes.summary: Directional tendency across timeframes; classified as uptrend, downtrend, or sideways/range.

nodes.label: Trend degrees
nodes.summary: Primary, intermediate, and minor trends should be aligned or explicitly separated.

nodes.label: Support and resistance
nodes.summary: Prior demand/supply zones where reactions, breakouts, failed breakouts, and polarity changes matter.

nodes.label: Trendlines and channels
nodes.summary: Visual tools for trend slope, boundary, acceleration, and breakout/reversal evidence.

nodes.label: Retracements
nodes.summary: Estimate pullback zones after directional moves; use as zones, not exact predictions.

nodes.label: Gaps
nodes.summary: Breakaway, runaway/measuring, exhaustion, and common gaps help diagnose trend strength or exhaustion.

nodes.label: Key reversal day
nodes.summary: A one-bar warning signal requiring context and confirmation, not a standalone reversal proof.

nodes.label: Major reversal patterns
nodes.summary: Structures suggesting trend change after a mature prior trend.

nodes.label: Head and shoulders
nodes.summary: Major reversal pattern with left shoulder, head, right shoulder, neckline, volume behavior, and measured target.

nodes.label: Double top/bottom
nodes.summary: Two-test reversal structure confirmed only after break of the intervening trough/peak.

nodes.label: Triple top/bottom
nodes.summary: Three-test reversal structure, often a variant of a rectangle or head-and-shoulders.

nodes.label: Rounding and saucer formations
nodes.summary: Slow reversal process; requires patience and participation confirmation.

nodes.label: Continuation patterns
nodes.summary: Pauses or consolidations that usually resolve in the direction of the prior trend.

nodes.label: Triangles
nodes.summary: Symmetrical, ascending, and descending compressions; breakout direction and volume matter.

nodes.label: Flags and pennants
nodes.summary: Short, sharp pauses after strong moves; often measured from the prior pole.

nodes.label: Rectangles
nodes.summary: Horizontal trading range; watch breakout, failed breakout, and polarity retests.

nodes.label: Wedges
nodes.summary: Contracting sloped structure; often resolves opposite the wedge slope, but context rules.

nodes.label: Volume and open-interest analysis
nodes.summary: Participation layer used to confirm or challenge price trend and pattern interpretation.

nodes.label: On-Balance Volume
nodes.summary: Cumulative volume indicator that should broadly confirm price direction or reveal divergence.

nodes.label: Long-term charts
nodes.summary: Higher timeframes provide context that daily charts can miss.

nodes.label: Market indices and baskets
nodes.summary: Broad indices can provide macro trend context and cross-market confirmation.

nodes.label: Moving averages
nodes.summary: Trend-following smoothing tools; best in persistent trends, whipsaw-prone in ranges.

nodes.label: Moving-average crossovers
nodes.summary: Lagging trend signals; use with structure, slope, and timeframe context.

nodes.label: Price channels
nodes.summary: Breakout rule based on recent high/low channels; useful for trend following.

nodes.label: Oscillators
nodes.summary: Momentum/range tools for overbought/oversold, divergence, and loss of momentum.

nodes.label: Momentum and rate of change
nodes.summary: Measures speed of price change and warns when momentum diverges from price.

nodes.label: Relative Strength Index
nodes.summary: Bounded oscillator for momentum, overbought/oversold, failure swings, and divergence.

nodes.label: Stochastic oscillator
nodes.summary: Compares close location to recent high-low range; useful in ranges and momentum shifts.

nodes.label: MACD
nodes.summary: EMA-based trend/momentum tool; histogram and signal-line behavior help assess momentum.

nodes.label: Divergence
nodes.summary: Price makes a new extreme while an indicator fails to confirm; warning, not immediate trigger.

nodes.label: Contrary opinion
nodes.summary: Extreme consensus can warn of crowded positioning and possible reversal.

nodes.label: Point and figure charting
nodes.summary: Filters time and small noise to focus on price reversals and count-based objectives.

nodes.label: Three-box reversal
nodes.summary: P&F method requiring a defined reversal amount before changing columns.

nodes.label: Elliott Wave
nodes.summary: Fractal wave-count framework for trend and correction structure; high discretion, needs validation.

nodes.label: Fibonacci
nodes.summary: Ratio framework often paired with Elliott waves and retracements/extensions.

nodes.label: Cycles
nodes.summary: Adds a time dimension to price analysis; useful only when phase and amplitude are plausible.

nodes.label: Computerized trading systems
nodes.summary: Computers help test and execute rules but do not replace sound technical judgment.

nodes.label: Money management
nodes.summary: Position size, stop placement, risk/reward, and survival are core parts of trading, not afterthoughts.

nodes.label: Trading tactics
nodes.summary: Entry, exit, stop, scaling, and order selection translate analysis into action.

nodes.label: Putting it all together
nodes.summary: Combine multiple partial clues instead of relying on any single method.

nodes.label: Spreads and options
nodes.summary: Technical analysis can inform related derivative strategies, but payoff and Greeks/change in spread structure must be considered.

nodes.label: Gann methods
nodes.summary: Historical technical toolkit; use cautiously and require independent confirmation.
